Discovered recently that eating my breakfast outside, in sunlight, within 45 mins of waking, has near 100% chance of ensuring my mood is at least 4/5 that day. For me this is huge.
I used to love lounging in bed in the morning; now I'm excited to get up as soon as I wake up. Stepping outside with my tea and yogurt feels amazing

I generally have a very varied mood, and can feel quite down especially in my luteal. Just before I tried this I experienced 2-3 days of feeling more depressed than I have in years - near-zero excitement or pleasure.

For me the craziest thing is how effortless and self-reinforcing this habit was - I literally started it when I was very ill, I now suspect with Covid. I had a fever for four days straight and still craved my sunny brekkie moment
I'd give a lot of money to have learned this 10 years ago
Rick rolled my bf today by overwriting his shower music. It’s the little things in life

AI psychosis might not be caused by AI, but it's indicative of an environment that's more likely to trigger your vulnerabilities than to help you grow into a stronger person

I worry about this as a general trend. I want a digital environment that makes us grow by default https://t.co/b3jbcTty9W

I'm really scared of sharing unfinished work but here goes: 

I'm working on a little project built on Community Archive, for searching across all users. The idea is that you can put very specific queries for people and the results will be so fitting it will feel like ✨ magic ✨ https://t.co/GyakVFT69W

Query examples:

People to follow: "wholesome and vibey analysis of world economy with a rationalist take"

Collaborators: "writer-philosopher to help with my network systems project"

Gigs: "an impressionist painter-photographer who could photograph AND paint our Oslo wedding"

I'm curious about the future of the job market, and this project explores 'Live Markets', drawing from Live Theory / autostructures. Some in my fellowship think this is just the natural continuation of the (human) economy: a better, worldwide offers/asks platform.
This project is just a demo/prototype - with a dataset of less than 300 people, it's not going to change the world. But I think it might be cool/useful for TPOT!

In particular, I would love to facilitate value creation from skills that normally aren't recognised as being of value in the job market. Aesthetic taste, skills from hobbies, knowledge of some niche science - in the right project, your idiosyncratic skillset might be key!

My DMs are open for thoughts/feedback and interested collaborators.

Also - You can be included in the dataset by contributing (uploading your tweets) to Community Archive!

https://t.co/rgnqJlYZSe

I feel like I’m getting more badass in my dreams as I get older?

As a kid I’d get eaten by the dragon and wake up scared and crying

@exgenesis Important q: is there going to be one on the 28th

I'm always doing things I don't actually know how to do...  I've never stuck with a tech stack long enough to feel like an expert

I started coding by making Android apps in my teens, knowing nothing but Java. Then became a game engineer, building features for a big online...

..mobile game in C# and Scala. Now I'm a web dev building AI tools

Always a beginner, but I kinda like it - I'm slowly making my way through all these different stacks, just following my nose. Learning every day, and gaining an aesthetic appreciation for each scene as I go

Related: I'm realising that I never learned how HTML forms really work, due to React abstracting or sidestepping them. Another +1 for Svelte for forcing me to learn this *basic thing* https://t.co/sZHQfWRg1k
